person_name: Proculus, Roman jurist or official (2nd century CE, contemporary of Fronto)
person_name_full: Proculus, Roman jurist or official (2nd century CE, contemporary of Fronto)
notes: Roman jurist or official in the 2nd century CE, contemporary of Fronto, noted for severity in passing sentence but otherwise easy-going.

docs.title: BOOK XV
docs.author: Ammianus Marcellinus
docs.excerpt: … been a servant of Silvanus, a man of weak body and of ill health; so that every one was afraid lest the exceeding violence of his torture should prove too much for his feeble limbs, so that he would expose numbers to be implicated in the accusations of atrocious crimes.

docs.title: Correspondence, Book Volume 2
docs.author: Fronto / Marcus Aurelius
docs.excerpt: … of a disposition in all other respects easy-going and pleasure-loving, yet in passing sentence was, I think, a little too ready to punish, and too severe . . . .

docs.title: Life of Tyranni Triginta
docs.author: Historia Augusta (anonymous)
docs.excerpt: … Proculus⁠ 77 the grammarian, the most learned man of his time, in the place where he tells of foreign countries. Saturninus 23 The best of the generals of the time of Gallienus, though, in fact, he was chosen by Valerian, was Saturninus.
